Hi! I'm Abhinav Kante — a Frontend Developer with a strong foundation in building responsive, user-friendly web interfaces. I'm experienced in integrating frontend components with backend APIs and delivering quality user experiences.

My journey spans Python, machine learning, data analysis, and web development. I completed a 6-week AICTE AI & ML internship with Edunet Foundation and IBM SkillsBuild, building real-world AI systems and earning credentials from IBM.

I believe in continuous learning — every project is an opportunity to grow. I'm actively looking for software development roles where I can bring clean code, analytical thinking, and a growth mindset to a great team.

Frontend Dev ML Practitioner Fast Learner Data-Driven Open Source 📍 Hyderabad

Projects

Things I've built

🌧️
01 / RESEARCH
Predicting Rainfall Using Machine Learning
Analyzed real-world weather datasets to identify key factors influencing rainfall. Designed, developed and tested ML models following SDLC practices. Improved prediction accuracy through iterative debugging, validation and optimization.
PythonScikit-learnPandasMatplotlib
View on GitHub →
🛡️
02 / CYBERSECURITY
Detecting Sybil Attacks in VANETs
Designed and implemented system logic to detect Sybil attacks using Proof of Work and location-based verification. Built backend components using Python and MySQL focusing on reliability and low latency overhead.
PythonMySQLPoW AlgorithmNetworking
View on GitHub →
📊
03 / ML + DEPLOYMENT
Employee Salary Classification
Built and compared multiple ML models (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, SVM) on the Adult dataset to classify income levels. Deployed as a live interactive web app using Streamlit.
StreamlitRandom ForestSVMGradient Boost
View on GitHub →
🎵
04 / AI + AUDIO
Live Audio Event Detection for Public Safety
Built an end-to-end ML system to classify emergency sounds — sirens, screams, explosions. Extracted MFCC, spectral contrast, and chroma features. Trained a LightGBM classifier with real-time inference via live microphone input.
PythonLightGBMMFCCAudio Processing
View on GitHub →
